message:
refactor: multi-volume ballast, inode-based open-file detection, Cow allocation reduction, and hardening

Major refactor spanning 10 modules with performance, correctness,
and security improvements:

Ballast (ballast/, daemon/):
- Replace single BallastManager with BallastPoolCoordinator for
  multi-volume ballast support; operations route through coordinator
  with per-volume targeting during pressure events
- Add Unix flock-based file locking in BallastManager to prevent
  concurrent access from multiple daemon instances
- Cache primary_path to avoid recomputing on every daemon tick

Walker (scanner/walker.rs, scanner/deletion.rs):
- Rewrite process_directory as single-pass with inline structural
  signal accumulation (eliminates two-pass collect-then-iterate)
- Rewrite collect_open_files to collect (device, inode) pairs via
  DirEntry::metadata instead of readlink on /proc/PID/fd symlinks;
  is_path_open now walks candidate tree checking inode tuples with
  20K-entry scan limit (fail conservative)
- Remove ~100 lines of duplicate open-file detection from deletion.rs;
  consolidate into walker module with batch collection per delete pass
- Add heartbeat callback for long-running walker threads
- Relax atomic ordering from SeqCst to Release/AcqRel/Acquire

Allocation Reduction (scanner/patterns.rs, scanner/scoring.rs):
- Change ArtifactClassification.pattern_name from String to
  Cow<'static, str>; built-in patterns use Cow::Borrowed (zero-alloc)
- Change CandidacyScore.veto_reason from Option<String> to
  Option<Cow<'static, str>>; static reasons use Cow::Borrowed
- Pre-compute lowercase_needle in NormalizedCustomPattern at
  registration time instead of per-classify call
- Use sort_unstable_by in batch_score

Security & Hardening:
- Atomic binary install via rename instead of in-place write
- Emergency mode requires explicit --yes in non-interactive contexts
  (piped stdin, cron) to prevent silent mass-deletion
- Escape AppleScript injection in macOS notifications
- Escape JSON control characters and add curl -- URL separator
  in webhook notifications

Bug Fixes:
- Fix /home veto: remove from system-path blocklist; explicitly allow
  /var/tmp and /dev/shm
- Fix ballast header size bounds check
- Use saturating_duration_since for PID controller clock safety
- Fix PressureReading/Response to carry mount PathBuf for targeted
  ballast release

Performance:
- Cache /proc/self/mounts with 5s TTL in LinuxPlatform
- Use prepare_cached for all SQLite logger operations
- Streaming SHA-256 in asset cache (prevents OOM on large files)
- VecDeque for MAPES window (O(1) pop_front vs O(n) remove(0))
- Thread VoiConfig.ewma_alpha into PathStats::update

Correctness:
- Hash paths via as_encoded_bytes in Merkle tree (preserves exact
  byte representation, avoids lossy UTF-8 hash collisions)
- Windows path normalization preserves root paths like C:\

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
